title: Pariti tricuspe
taxonomy: Pariti tricuspe is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae, and is native to India and Sri Lanka. It is classified under the subfamily Faboideae. The genus Pariti is a part of the tribe Millettieae. Pariti tricuspe belongs to the family of flowering plants known as angiosperms. This classification places it in the broader group of flowering plants, which are a key part of the plant kingdom.

intro: Pariti tricuspe, also known as the "Three-horned Pariti," is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as fever, cough, and skin infections.
